title: Emotional Agility: Get Unstuck, Embrace Change, and Thrive in Work and Life
subtitle: A modern, evidence-based approach to emotional intelligence — learning to navigate emotions with flexibility, courage, and intentionality.
author: Susan David
themes: Emotions are data, not directives — they inform but should not dictate behavior
The four-step emotional agility process: Showing Up, Stepping Out, Walking Your Why, Moving On
The difference between hooking (being controlled by emotions) and unhooking (choosing responses)
Values-based action as the alternative to emotion-driven reactivity
The courage to feel difficult emotions rather than suppressing or avoiding them
awards: Harvard Business Review Idea of the Year (2016)
Best Book of 2016 — Forbes, Financial Times, Inc.
excerpt: Harvard Medical School psychologist Susan David offers a new paradigm for emotional intelligence: emotional agility. The ability to recognize your emotions, accept them, and choose how to respond — rather than being hooked by them — is the key to resilience, well-being, and success. David's four-step framework — Showing Up, Stepping Out, Walking Your Why, and Moving On — provides a practical path from emotional reactivity to intentional action.
